---
title: "Stata-Only Analysis"
author: "Your Name"
date: today
format: html
engine: knitr
---

## Overview

This document demonstrates a **Stata-only** Quarto workflow using the knitr
execution engine. All code cells run in Stata; no R or Python chunks are used.

> **Prerequisites**
> - R ≥ 4.0 with the `knitr` package installed (`install.packages("knitr")`)
> - Stata 17 or later
> - Update `engine.path` below to match your Stata installation

---

## Setup

Configure knitr to locate your Stata executable. This chunk runs once and
applies settings to all subsequent chunks.

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = TRUE,
  comment = "",
  engine.path = list(
    stata = "/path/to/stata"
    # Linux / macOS : "/usr/local/stata18/stata-mp"
    #               : "/usr/local/stata18/stata-se"
    # Windows       : "C:/Program Files/Stata18/StataMP-64.exe"
    # If `stata` is already on PATH, you can remove engine.path entirely.
  )
)
```

---

## Load Data

```{stata load-data}
sysuse auto, clear
describe
```

## Summary Statistics

```{stata summary}
summarize price mpg weight length foreign
```

## Tabulation

```{stata tab}
tabulate foreign, missing
```

## Regression

```{stata regression}
regress price mpg weight i.foreign
```

## Scatter Plot

Graphs exported by Stata are embedded automatically when using Quarto + knitr.

```{stata scatter, fig.cap="Relationship between fuel efficiency and price"}
scatter price mpg, ///
    title("Price vs. Mileage") ///
    xtitle("Mileage (mpg)")    ///
    ytitle("Price ($)")        ///
    name(scatter_price_mpg, replace)

graph export scatter_price_mpg.png, replace
```

---

## Positron IDE Note

When opening this file in Positron with the `positron-stata` extension active,
you can execute cells interactively using the running Stata session — no knitr
configuration is needed for the interactive preview path.
